notes:
The main issue as seen in the log files (located at "steam_lib/steamapps/compatdata/381210/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/AppData/Roaming/EasyAntiCheat") is that the CDN seems to be failing to download:



83/loader.log:

[2022.05.07-16.31.27] Loader initialized.

[2022.05.07-16.31.27] [Connection] Connecting to URL: https://download.eac-cdn.com/api/v1/games/83/client/wine64/download/?uuid=d35905f8-4c0b-1f0e-268e-aca0588aa761

...

[2022.05.07-16.31.27] Download Progress: 0%.

Destination IP: 52.85.151.29

[2022.05.07-16.31.27] HTTP Response Code: 200 Curl Code: 0

[2022.05.07-16.31.27] Falling back to a secondary CDN path.

[2022.05.07-16.31.27] Unexpected error. (Incorrect CDN url)



Despite the loading bar filling up, no progress was made in the main log file (gamelauncher.log) either.

I'm still messing with EAC's Settings.json files. (I switched the escaped forward slashes to backslashes in the executable paths for the EAC settings files. No idea if this made a difference, but probably not.)

notes: Unfortunately, every time you try to get into a match, you'll be met with failure. After the countdown finishes, you'll be greeted by an error saying that you were disconnected from the host. Sometimes, this happens after offerings are burnt (and lost). Sometimes, this happens before the offering screen.
title: Dead by Daylight

notes:
I always start everything on Experimental so no true tinkering on my side.

The game finally lets you in. The error occurs past successful lobby when you try to load in - it fails to connect to the host. I (fortunately) don't have friends who play DBD so I couldn't test if custom works, but at the very least you can get into the menus now.

I tried survivor with crossplay - error.
Survivor without crossplay - failed to find a lobby to start a game.
Killer without crossplay - failed to find a lobby to start a game.
Killer with crossplay - I saw the loading started, but I got 2 errors and it didn't load in.
Maybe a Linux only lobby could start? No idea.

Note: game seems to have considerably better performance (FPS) in the menus via Proton than on Windows, but I was kept out of the game via EAC for so long it might be from an update rather than Proton vs Windows.
title: Dead by Daylight
